name: induce_override_phase_a
description: Phase A ablation matrix for the induce_override rule. TieredBot (hero) vs four rule_bot villains (ManiacBot, CaseBot, GTO-Lite, ABCBot/TAG). Two variants per villain: rule ON and rule OFF (ablated via TieredBotController.disable_rules). Tests whether smooth-calling instead of raising with nuts on flop/turn facing-bet IP improves bb/100 vs barrelers without leaking against non-barrelers.
hypothesis: (H1) +5 bb/100 lift vs ManiacBot when rule is ON. (H2) <=-2 bb/100 leak vs each non-barreler. (H3) <1% fire rate vs ABCBot/TAG. All three must hold; any miss kills Phase A.
_note_starting_stack: 200 BB starting stack ensures the 20 BB effective-stack gate is satisfied for the great majority of hands. 500 hands x 4 seeds = 2000 hands per arm.
_note_seeding: random_seed=1001 is the experiment-level base seed. Each tournament uses base + tournament_index so seed variance across the 4 tournaments produces independent samples.
_note_wiring: This config depends on TieredBotController accepting a disable_rules key from player_type_config (currently set programmatically only). Wiring this through run_ai_tournament.py:944 is part of the Phase A implementation work — see docs/plans/INDUCE_OVERRIDE_PHASE_A.md 'Pipeline integration'.

_kpi_computation.primary: bb_per_100 = (final_stack - starting_stack) / big_blind / hands * 100, aggregated mean +/- stderr across the 4 tournaments per variant

pass_conditions.H3: induce_fire_rate < 0.01 in arms C2 and D2 (from snapshot counters)

_kpi_computation.key_diagnostic: induce_followup_barrel_rate vs ManiacBot is the empirical check on the poker premise. If <0.4 with H1 missing, the proxy signal is wrong (jump to Phase B). If >0.6 with H1 missing, the redistribution ratio is wrong (one retune allowed, then kill).
